# Drone News 48h Brief — March 23, 2026

_Window: last 48 hours; 67 incidents._

## Summary
In the past 48 hours, significant drone-related incidents have occurred primarily in the Middle East, particularly surrounding Dubai, leading to widespread airport disruptions. A notable drone attack resulted in Emirates Flight from Edinburgh to Dubai being diverted, alongside multiple shutdowns affecting thousands of travelers at Dubai and Abu Dhabi airports. Additional incidents included new drone attacks near Pulkovo Airport in St. Petersburg, contributing to further flight cancellations. Security threats manifested with breaches at U.S. nuclear bomber bases due to unauthorized drone swarms. Arrests linked to drone-related incidents such as smuggling and attacks highlight continuing public safety and border security concerns across various regions. Overall, drone activities reaffirm the urgent need for enhanced security measures at critical infrastructure and airports.

**By category:** airport: 15 • arrest: 4 • crash: 3 • interference: 2 • other: 17 • security: 14 • smuggling: 6 • surveillance: 1 • terrorism: 5
**Top queries:** drone incident (16), drone airport disruption (13), drone security breach (9), drone terrorism (7), unauthorized drone (7)

## Clusters
**Airport disruptions** — High frequency of drone incidents leading to flight cancellations and diversions.

## Highlights
- **[Emirates Flight from Edinburgh Diverted](https://news.google.?oc=5)** — A drone attack caused Emirates Flight from Edinburgh to be diverted over Egypt, highlighting airport security vulnerabilities. _(score 0.80; tags: interference, airport)_
- **[Dubai Airport Shutdown After Drone Strike](https://news.google.?oc=5)** — A drone strike caused significant disruptions at Dubai airport, affecting thousands of passengers. _(score 0.90; tags: airport, interference)_
